markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define allowlist rules for project roots, worktrees, external docs, media folders, and denied machine-local paths. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: ProjectRootAllowlist leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ProjectRootAllowlist is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 c61465c2; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: Default deny patterns (allowlist.rs DEFAULT_DENY_PATTERNS) cover .git/.ssh/secrets/node_modules/target/managed_pgdata but NOT common secret-bearing files like **/.env*, **/*.pem, **/id_rsa, **/.aws/**, **/.npmrc — defense-in-depth gap; relies on the MT-091 file-level secret preflight as the backstop (which is sound but pattern-limited, see MT-091).

register_root persists the policy DECISION row and EventLedger receipt BEFORE checking is_allowed() and returning PolicyDenied — correct (denials are durable evidence), but the source root row is only created on allow; verified no Ok-path leak.
evasion_probes: empty allow_patterns -> DeniedNotAllowlisted (fail-closed) CONFIRMED via allowlist.rs test empty_allow_list_fails_closed and code path evaluate().
deny-wins-over-allow CONFIRMED (deny matches checked first in evaluate()).
malformed glob -> typed Validation error, never silent-allow CONFIRMED (CompiledRootPolicy::compile).
operator-gated kinds (ExternalImport/OperatorFolder) always require operator_approved CONFIRMED.
candidate path normalized via storage::knowledge::normalize_repo_relative_path before evaluation — drive-letter/UNC/traversal rejected upstream (see MT-083).
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_ingestion.rs:300-341 register_root handler (403 typed denial w/ decision_id)

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (8 hardening tests + 70 unit, no regression).

evidence: Hardened 5876feaf: ingestion secret-leak/lopdf/dead-letter fixes verified; boundary-seam fixture shows ZERO raw secret bytes in spans; DB redaction guard (0210) + receipt NOT NULL (0211) enforced.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-081 requires a workspace-level project-root registration allowlist (deny-wins, fail-closed empty allow list, operator-approval gate, secret-aware default deny patterns) with PostgreSQL/EventLedger durable authority for every allow/deny decision. The code implements this fully: a typed compiled glob policy, runtime enforcement in the ingestion engine that persists a durable decision row plus an EventLedger receipt before returning a typed PolicyDenied, and real-PostgreSQL integration tests proving the allow path, typed denial with durable receipt, deny-wins, operator gates, and DB constraints.
findings: allowlist.rs:31-48 DEFAULT_DENY_PATTERNS denies .git/.ssh/secrets/node_modules/target/managed_pgdata AND the hardening item #10 secret-file shapes (**/.env*, **/*.pem, **/id_rsa*, **/.aws/**, **/.npmrc) - the prior adversarial concern (ingestion_hardening #10, commit 5876feaf) is fixed in code.

allowlist.rs:187-230 evaluate() enforces correct order: deny patterns first (deny-wins), then allow-or-fail-closed (empty allow list -> DeniedNotAllowlisted), then operator approval (policy flag OR operator-gated root kind ExternalImport/OperatorFolder). No placeholders, todo!(), or deferred-live escapes anywhere in the module (grep clean).

engine.rs:145-229 register_root persists a durable knowledge_ingestion_policy_decisions row AND an EventLedger receipt (append_receipt_event -> kernel_event_ledger) for EVERY evaluation (allow and deny) BEFORE returning a typed IngestionError::PolicyDenied carrying decision_id; allow path creates the source root only after Allowed verdict. This satisfies sub-rule 2 (real Handshake-owned PostgreSQL/EventLedger touch).

migrations/0160_knowledge_ingestion_policies.sql:26-95 defines knowledge_ingestion_root_policies (single-ACTIVE partial unique index, retained superseded rows) + knowledge_ingestion_policy_decisions with portable-path CHECK, id-format CHECK, and a real FK to kernel_event_ledger(event_id) - durable, replayable authority, not prose.

tests/knowledge_ingestion_policy_tests.rs:42-343 are real isolated-schema PostgreSQL integration tests: allowed_registration_persists_root_decision_and_ledger_receipt asserts the kernel_event_ledger row payload; unallowlisted_root_is_rejected_with_typed_error_and_durable_receipt asserts typed PolicyDenied + durable FK'd denial decision + receipt; deny_patterns_and_operator_approval_gates_enforce and the DB-constraint probes (partial unique index, id CHECK, portable-path CHECK) all touch the real cluster. Tests skip gracefully when PG absent but packet recovery_snapshot + commit 58b2af10 record them green on the Handshake-managed cluster.

store.rs:210-258 record_policy_decision / list_policy_decisions are real parameterized SQL against the pool - no mock/in-memory stand-in. Aside (non-blocking, paperwork only): MT-081.json spec_anchors and the migration comment cite bundle v02.192; current bundle is v02.194 - the same section 2.3.13.11 (KnowledgeSource with permission scope/redaction state) exists and supports the allowlist, so this is stale-pointer drift, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: wtc-kernel-009 git commits c61465c2 (MT-081), 58b2af10 (MT-093 shared runtime+tests), 5876feaf (hardening)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
